title: Scan agent subprocess OUTPUT for injection and secrets before it re-enters the loop
source: OWASP LLM Top 10 (2025) — LLM05 Improper Output Handling & LLM02 Sensitive Information Disclosure; codebase gap: delegate.py:2630 treats agent stdout as trusted
description: Incoming ticket descriptions are scanned (delegate.py:1581) but Morty agent OUTPUT is only path/status-sanitized, never scanned for prompt injection or leaked secrets. A compromised or manipulated agent can emit injection payloads or exfiltrated credentials that are logged to ticket JSON and fed to downstream reviewer/reviewer agents — closing an untrusted feedback loop. Treat agent output as untrusted per OWASP LLM05.
proposed_changes: In delegate.py where parse_agent_output()/parsed is produced (~line 2630), run detect_injection_patterns() and redact_secrets() over the raw subprocess stdout BEFORE it is saved to the ticket, logged via append_log (~2864), or passed to another agent. On injection detection, call audit_log_security_event('agent_output_injection', ...) and mark the ticket for human review rather than auto-advancing. Add a helper sanitize_agent_stdout(text) in security_utils.py that returns (clean_text, findings) combining redact_secrets + detect_injection_patterns in 'warn' mode so it never hard-fails a legit ticket.
